title: olearia rugosa subsp. rugosa
intro: Olearia rugosa subsp. rugosa, also known as the woolly tree daisy or woolly bush daisy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Olearia rugosa subsp. rugosa can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
